cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The question is about why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. The options are A, B, C, D, each giving a different reason. First, I need to find the relevant part of the passage. The passage mentions events around 1828. Let me go through the text again to find that section. In the third paragraph, it says: "Then his health broke down, and he had to take a long holiday abroad. When he returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done." So, when he returned in 1828, he was out of resources (money), had unpaid bills, and had conflicts with his co-workers. The question is asking the main reason he couldn't continue his research. The options are: A) he was in poor health B) he almost ran out of money C) his co-workers argued with him D) he spent all his money on his bills The passage says that when he returned in 1828, he was "at the end of his resources," which means he was out of money (B). The other issues, like health breakdown, happened before
